skill.name: create-plan
skill.description: Create structured implementation plans in a .plans directory for features, refactors, fixes, and enhancements after reviewing requirements and code context.
skill.summary: Generates thorough implementation plans with requirement coverage, impact analysis, removal notes, and validation checks.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found markdown examples, file extensions, and planning templates rather than executable code. The skill writes planning documents and may suggest standard developer commands, so it is low risk with normal user review of generated plans and commands.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Write Guidance for Plan Files
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to create plan documents in a .plans directory. This is the intended behavior, but users should review generated files before acting on them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines explicitly describe creating .plans files and reporting their location. This is a confirmed filesystem capability, but it is not malicious.

low_findings.title: Benign External Command Suggestions
low_findings.description: Static command detections are markdown examples or validation commands such as GitHub issue lookup, grep, TypeScript, linting, and tests. They are not hidden scripts and do not execute automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command references are visible in markdown documentation and validation checklists. No executable wrapper, shell script, or dynamic command construction was found.

low_findings.title: Template Placeholders Triggered Static Patterns
low_findings.description: Template lines contain placeholder file paths and validation checklist text. These explain many static findings and do not show command execution or cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited template sections are documentation placeholders, not executable code. They match the static locations but lack a runtime path or attacker-controlled input.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Detections
dangerous_patterns.description: The high-severity weak cryptography detections correspond to markdown file extensions, headings, and placeholder text. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage was found in the reviewed files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found natural-language planning content at these locations, not cryptographic operations. This makes the scanner result a high-confidence false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Reconnaissance Detections
dangerous_patterns.description: The reconnaissance detections map to planning and validation language, not scanning hosts, enumerating networks, or collecting system inventory. No evidence found of network or system reconnaissance behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The relevant text describes implementation anti-patterns and validation requirements. It does not include probes, scans, network destinations, or data collection logic.

content.user_title: Create Implementation Plans for Code Changes
content.value_statement: Complex code changes fail when requirements, impact, and validation are unclear. This skill turns feature, fix, and refactor requests into structured plans that teams can review before implementation.
actual_capabilities: Maps original issue requirements to specific plan steps.
Prompts review of codebase context, CLAUDE.md guidance, existing types, and dependencies.
Structures plans with context, impact analysis, implementation steps, removal notes, and validation criteria.
Provides separate templates for feature, fix, and refactor planning.
Includes validation checklists for TypeScript, linting, tests, and dead code removal.
limitations: It creates plans, not the final implementation.
Plan quality depends on the issue details and codebase context provided.
GitHub issue lookup requires the GitHub CLI to be available and authenticated.
The templates may need adjustment for non-TypeScript or non-Claude projects.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a feature request into a reviewable plan with requirements, affected files, implementation steps, and validation checks.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Bug Fix
use_cases.description: Document the bug, root cause, affected code paths, repair steps, safeguards, and regression tests before changing code.

use_cases.title: Coordinate a Refactor
use_cases.description: Map existing types, dependencies, files to modify, files to delete, and cleanup steps for a controlled refactor.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a short task description.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a plan for this change: add user profile editing. Include requirements, affected areas, implementation steps, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan from an Issue
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when an issue or ticket includes acceptance criteria.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation plan from issue 42. Extract every requirement, map each one to plan steps, and save the plan in .plans.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Bug Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the root cause is known or needs to be documented.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a fix plan for the login timeout bug. Include current behavior, expected behavior, root cause, affected paths, test cases, and removal notes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Refactor
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for larger code cleanup work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a refactor plan for the payment service. Analyze existing types, dependencies, files to consolidate, files to delete, validation steps, and migration risks.

output_examples.input: Plan a feature to add account deletion.
output_examples.output: A todo plan document with a summary, source requirements, context, affected files, implementation steps, removal notes, and validation checklist.

output_examples.input: Create a fix plan for failed password reset emails.
output_examples.output: A fix-focused plan covering the bug description, root cause, impacted code paths, repair steps, safeguards, and regression tests.

output_examples.input: Plan a refactor of the notification module.
output_examples.output: A refactor plan that identifies reusable types, dependencies, files to change, files to remove, cleanup steps, and behavior validation.

best_practices: Provide the original issue, acceptance criteria, and known edge cases before asking for a plan.
Let the skill inspect relevant code, CLAUDE.md files, existing types, and dependencies before drafting steps.
Review the removal specification and validation checklist before starting implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill when the change is small enough for a direct edit.
Do not accept generated plans without checking affected files and missing requirements.
Do not run suggested shell commands without reviewing what they do first.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured implementation plans in a .plans directory for features, fixes, refactors, and enhancements.

faq.question: Does it write production code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on planning, requirements coverage, impact analysis, and validation steps before implementation.

faq.question: Can it use GitHub issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide use of the GitHub CLI to read an issue when the CLI is available and authenticated.

faq.question: What files does it include?
faq.answer: It includes the main skill instructions and templates for feature, fix, and refactor plans.

faq.question: Why does the audit mention external commands?
faq.answer: The instructions include visible developer commands for issue lookup and validation. They are examples, not hidden executable code.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Developers, maintainers, and technical leads who want a reviewable plan before making a code change.
